functional_requirements.requirement: Users can browse products through a catalog.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: users can browse products through a catalog.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can view items directly or explore different ways of browsing in the catalog.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: they can view items directly or explore different ways of browsing.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can browse products using an index, either by browsing alphabetically or by category.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: users can look through products using an index, either by browsing alphabetically or by category.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can switch between browsing options depending on their preference.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: they can switch between these options depending on how they prefer to search.

functional_requirements.requirement: Once a user finds an interesting product, they can select it to view its details.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: once they find something interesting, they can select a product to view its details.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can go to their basket at any time to review the items they have selected.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: the user can choose to go to their basket to review the items they have selected.

functional_requirements.requirement: From the basket, users can either return to the catalog or move forward to checkout.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: from there, they can either return to the catalog to continue browsing or move forward to checkout.

functional_requirements.requirement: During checkout, users review their selections and proceed with the purchase.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: during checkout, the user reviews their selections and proceeds with the purchase.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can go back to the catalog from the checkout page if they need to make changes before completing the process.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: if needed, they can still go back to the catalog to make changes before completing the process.

prompt:
You are a requirements engineer.
Extract functional software requirements from the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "functional_requirements": [
    {
      "requirement": "string",
      "evidence_quote": "short exact quote from source text"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
1. Include only system capabilities/behaviors.
2. Exclude background context, impact statements, problem motivation, and pure business goals.
3. Keep each requirement atomic and clear.
4. Preserve original meaning. Do not invent functionality.
5. If uncertain, keep the item but keep evidence_quote explicit.

Source text:
<<<
In an online shopping system, users can browse products through a catalog. When they enter the catalog, they can view items directly or explore different ways of browsing.

Users can look through products using an index, either by browsing alphabetically or by category. They can switch between these options depending on how they prefer to search. Once they find something interesting, they can select a product to view its details.

At any time, the user can choose to go to their basket to review the items they have selected. From there, they can either return to the catalog to continue browsing or move forward to checkout.

During checkout, the user reviews their selections and proceeds with the purchase. If needed, they can still go back to the catalog to make changes before completing the process.

This setup allows users to smoothly move between browsing products, viewing details, managing their basket, and completing their purchase in a flexible and user-friendly way.
>>>
